summary: Session 1 of 4: completion-detection model + dual-mode Explorer shell. Shipped (1) a pure, VS Code-free, fs-injected completion-detection module (src/utils/gettingStartedDetection.ts) implementing D3 — detectCompletion {structureBuilt, planPresent, sessionSetsPresent}, selectExplorerMode (D1/D5), and the host-composition helper computeGettingStarted — plus a nodeDetectionFs adapter; (2) dual-mode wiring in CustomSessionSetsView (buildGettingStarted -> SnapshotPayload.gettingStarted) and the ExplorerMode/GettingStartedPayload protocol additions; (3) the webview client.js dual-mode surfaces — a no-folder CTA and the 3-step Getting Started form shell (Full/Lightweight radio + parallel checkbox) greyed/checked per live D3 state, with buttons INERT this session (data-gs-action, not data-command); tree.css form styles; (4) a Getting Started live-progress file watcher in extension.ts covering all D3 inputs (engine files, .venv ai_router package, project-plan.md, numbered set dirs) so the form's steps grey/check live. 16 new unit tests; full unit suite 660 passing / 2 pre-existing stub-harness failures (configEditor ViewColumn + notifications, both unrelated to this diff — ViewColumn is undefined in the vscode-stub). esbuild compile clean. No release this session (0.29.0 bump is Session 4).
verification_notes: Cross-provider verification routed to gpt-5-4 (OpenAI) over 4 rounds, cumulative $0.514. R1 found 3 issues: (Issue 2, Major) exists() accepted a DIRECTORY named like CLAUDE.md/project-plan.md -> FIXED with fileExists = exists && !isDirectory + regression tests; (Issue 3, Minor) protocol said gettingStarted always-present-or-null while client handles undefined -> FIXED to optional; (Issue 1) mode flip keyed on hasAnySets not the bare NNN- dir probe -> DISPOSITIONED as intentional (D1 = a renderable set requires spec.md; flipping on a bare dir would render an empty list) with a code comment; R2 confirmed all three resolved/acceptable but raised a completeness gap (form live-progress inputs not watched) -> FIXED by adding a getting-started watcher; R3 wanted the .venv router probe and bare numbered-dir also watched -> FIXED by broadening the single watcher to cover ALL D3 inputs (rides VS Code's existing recursive workspace watcher, no new OS watch); R4 returned VERIFIED. watcherInventory convention test updated (new callsite allowlisted with D1 rationale; baseline 1 -> 2).

reason.specifics: Session 2 wires the three form actions (build-structure / import-plan / build-session-sets) onto the data-gs-action hooks this session left inert, reusing the same scaffolder/planning/decomposition engines; same orchestrator keeps the dual-mode rendering and protocol context with no provider switch warranted.
